title: Tapping for Health Anxiety
subtitle: An EFT sequence for the fear that something is physically wrong
excerpt: An EFT tapping practice for health anxiety: the persistent worry that something is wrong with the body, the checking and rechecking, the spiral from a symptom to a worst case. Works through the anxiety pattern toward a calmer relationship with physical uncertainty.
sourceNotes: Written for homemade.education. EFT framework developed by Gary Craig (mid-1990s). Health anxiety (also known as illness anxiety) is a recognised anxiety presentation.

practice.whenToUse: Use when health anxiety is up: when a symptom has triggered a spiral, when you have been checking and rechecking, or when the body's ordinary sensations are being read as threats.

content.text: A five-minute EFT tapping practice for health anxiety. The script works through the specific pattern of fear, checking, and catastrophic interpretation of physical symptoms, toward a place where the body's ordinary uncertainty is more tolerable.
New to tapping? Read
how tapping works
first.
The practice
Karate chop
Tap the karate chop point and say each statement once:
Even though I am afraid that something is physically wrong and I can't stop thinking about it, I deeply and completely accept myself.
Even though my mind goes to the worst case and I can't talk it out of it, I am open to letting the fear ease.
Even though the body's uncertainty is very hard to tolerate, I accept that I am doing the best I can with it.
Tapping round
Tap each point seven times while saying the phrase:
Eyebrow: The symptom I noticed that started the spiral.
Side of Eye: The worst case my mind went to.
Under Eye: Checking and rechecking and not feeling reassured.
Under Nose: The way the anxiety makes me scan the body for more evidence.
Chin: Being inside a body I can't fully know or control.
Collarbone: The fear that if I stop worrying I'll miss something important.
Under Arm: How much energy this takes.
Top of Head: The impossibility of knowing for certain that everything is fine.
Reframe to positive
Eyebrow: The body has managed much. It is more robust than the anxiety says.
Side of Eye: The worst case is one possibility. It is not a certainty.
Under Eye: Checking keeps the anxiety alive. Pausing is allowed.
Under Nose: The anxiety is not information about the body. It is information about the anxiety.
Chin: I can tolerate not knowing. I have been doing it.
Collarbone: I can attend to the body without being ruled by it.
Under Arm: I give myself permission to put this down for now.
Top of Head: Right now, in this moment, I am here.
Tap each round three times, then move to the reframe and tap that three times.
Where this practice comes from
Written for homemade.education. The EFT framework is Gary Craig's Emotional Freedom Technique, in use since the mid-1990s.
